mainmatter.p: We will often write \operatorname {ker} {f} \xrightarrow { i _{ \operatorname {ker} { f } } }A for the kernel of a map A \xrightarrow {f}B in a category with zero morphisms \mathcal {A}.

A category with zero morphisms is defined to be a category enriched in the category of pointed sets and strict maps. The distinguished point of \mathbf {hom} _{ \mathcal {A} } { \mathopen {} \left ( A , B \right ) \mathclose {}} shall be written 0.

Let \mathcal {A} be a category with zero morphisms. Then the kernel of a morphism A \xrightarrow {f} B in \mathcal {A} is defined to be a morphism K \xrightarrow {k}{A} such that for any X \xrightarrow {h}A with h;f=0, there exists a unique map X \xrightarrow {}K factoring X \xrightarrow {h}A through K \xrightarrow {k}{A} in the sense of the diagram below:
